name: Camporredondo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Camporredondo de Alba y Triollo – circular por el Embalse de Camporredondo
description: Cycle a moderate 17.9-mile (28.9 km) loop around Embalse de Camporredondo, enjoying mountain views and historical paths in Montaña Palentina
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Velilla Del Río Carrión traverse a landscape characterized by river valleys, reservoirs, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. The region features varied terrain, including moderate ascents and descents, with routes often following the Carrión River. Cyclists can expect views of natural water bodies and surrounding forested areas. The area provides a mix of challenging touring bike trails and more accessible paths suitable for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Velilla Del Río Carrión The most popular touring cycling route is Camporredondo Reservoir Loop, a moderate 17.9 miles (28.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Camporredondo Reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Compuerto Reservoir Loop, a difficult 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. It features challenging climbs and panoramic views of the Compuerto Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Guardo, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail leading through the rio carrion valley, often completed in about 4 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling around Velilla Del Río Carrión is defined by river valleys, reservoir loops, and mountain passes, offering scenic viewpoints Velilla Del Río Carrión bike routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to challenging touring bike trails. The routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 120 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Velilla Del Río Carrión's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes documented on komoot around Velilla Del Río Carrión, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners?
A: While the region is known for its varied terrain, including moderate ascents and descents, the easiest routes available are generally classified as moderate. These routes still offer a rewarding experience without extreme difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170973762/camporredondo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camporredondo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.9-mile trail that provides scenic views.

Q: What kind of scenic views can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Velilla Del Río Carrión are characterized by river valleys, reservoirs, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. You can expect views of natural water bodies like the Camporredondo and Compuerto Reservoirs, as well as surrounding forested areas. Many routes offer panoramic vistas, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170984308/compuerto-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Compuerto Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, the region offers several challenging routes. Over half of the routes are classified as difficult, featuring significant climbs and descents. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Guardo&lt;/a&gt;, a 26-mile trail that leads through the Carrión river valley and includes substantial elevation changes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Many of the touring cycling routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170973762/camporredondo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camporredondo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1170984308/compuerto-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Compuerto Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic circular journeys.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks along the routes?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty, particularly lakes and mountain passes. You can encounter highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5840202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camporredondo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Compuerto Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Lomas&lt;/a&gt;. For mountain views, consider routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3593173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Glorio Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3533674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which offer stunning vistas like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/814184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Picos de Europa from Collado de Llesba&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Velilla Del Río Carrión is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically milder and more stable, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be warm, especially in the valleys, while winters can bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that follow the Carrión River?
A: Yes, the Carrión River is a prominent feature of the region, and several routes follow its course or traverse its valley. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24633375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guardo – Carrión River in Guardo loop from Guardo&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a scenic ride along the river.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión?
A: The touring cycling routes in Velilla Del Río Carrión are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful reservoir views, and the opportunities for both moderate and challenging rides through the natural landscape.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those seeking extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14987855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Picones (1316 m) – Velilla del Río Carrión loop from Guardo Apeadero&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 50 kilometers, offering a substantial touring experience with significant elevation.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes?
A: Public transport options directly to the trailheads might be limited in this rural region. It is advisable to check local bus schedules for connections to Velilla Del Río Carrión or nearby towns like Guardo, which serve as starting points for some routes. Planning your journey in advance is recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking for the cycling routes?
A: Parking is generally available in the towns and villages that serve as starting points for the routes, such as Velilla Del Río Carrión itself, Guardo, or near major attractions like the reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31852292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Triollo Village – Alto de La Varga Viewpoint loop from Parking Pinollano&lt;/a&gt; explicitly mentions a parking area at its starting point.
tips: Excellent viewpoint with an impressive panoramic view of the Camporredondo reservoir and its surroundings. Located in a privileged natural environment, the viewpoint allows you to enjoy the beauty of the mountainous landscape and the crystalline reservoir.

The Camporredondo reservoir regulates the course of the Carrión river and creates an irrigable area of great importance for the province. Built in 1930, the reservoir is part of the "Ruta de los Pantanos" together with the reservoirs of Aguilar de Campoo, Ruesga, Requejada and Compuerto.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While much of the climbing is progressive, there are segments with more pronounced inclines, including a 'hard ramp' spanning almost a kilometer. It requires a good level of physical fitness due to its varied terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Camporredondo de Alba y Triollo circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, primarily consisting of mostly paved tracks suitable for touring cyclists. However, it also includes well-marked dirt tracks and forest paths, so you should expect some off-road sections. Parts of the route follow the ancient 'Camino Real', which indicates a relatively established path.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be treated to spectacular natural landscapes, including incomparable views of the prominent peaks Espigüete and Curavacas. The path winds through dense broom scrubland and hills covered in yellow-flowered gorse, and a section passes through a shady pine reforestation. The Camporredondo Reservoir is a central feature, offering picturesque water views. Don't miss the View of Camporredondo Reservoir from Mirador de Alba de los Cardaños, and the unique visual of the 1930 dam's spillway in Camporredondo de Alba during discharge periods.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 28.8 kilometers long and typically takes around 2 hours and 14 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Camporredondo de Alba y Triollo circular route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can generally find parking in the villages of Camporredondo de Alba or Triollo, which serve as common starting points for routes around the reservoir. Look for designated parking spots or suitable roadside areas.

faqs.question: Is this route family-friendly or suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, varied terrain including dirt tracks and some steep climbs, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or very young children. It requires a good level of physical condition and some experience with touring cycling on mixed surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically during spring, early summer, or autumn when the weather is milder. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Avoid peak summer heat or winter snow and ice, which can make parts of the trail challenging or inaccessible.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a substantial portion of this route coincides with other significant trails. It forms part of the ancient 'Camino Real' and also integrates with sections of the GR-1 long-distance trail, known as the 'Sendero Histórico'. Other intersecting trails include the Asturian Way, Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, Camino Real Antiguo de Camporredondo de Alba a la Lastra, Camino de Lamas, and Camino de los Canales.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes are not provided. As a general rule in natural parks like Montaña Palentina, it's advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in Parque Natural Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the Parque Natural Montaña Palentina. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of protected areas and private property.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Camporredondo de Alba and Triollo. These villages may offer limited options for cafes or restaurants, especially during peak season. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, as services can be sparse along the trail itself.

waypoints.name: View of Camporredondo Reservoir from Mirador de Alba de los Cardaños
